prose:
Method–provenance summary (DFT Fukui analysis on furan)

• Property: condensed Fukui functions f⁺ (electrophilic attack), f⁻ (nucleophilic attack), dual descriptor (Δf = f⁺ – f⁻) for every atom; identification of the most electrophilic/nucleophilic site.  
• Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (exact four-center integrals, no density fitting).  
• Geometry provenance: fixed input coordinates supplied by user (/home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/furan.xyz); single-point population analyses on this geometry (no further optimisation: DFT//input-xyz).  
• Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
• Charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell singlet for the neutral state).  
  – Cation state: charge +1, multiplicity 2.  
  – Anion  state: charge –1, multiplicity 2.  
• Software: PySCF 2.4.0 via chemkit `fukui-reactivity` skill (engine sub-command `fukui`).  
• Convergence & integrity: SCF cycles for N, N±1 states all converged; integrity.status = “ok”, trustworthy = true.

Warnings (verbatim):
1. sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. common header present

Key numerical results (Mulliken charges → Fukui functions):
Atom  f⁺      f⁻      Δf
1 O  −1.33162  0.083822  −1.41544
2 C   1.22869  0.089103   1.13958
3 C   1.22703  0.089305   1.13772
4 C   1.92331  0.161527   1.76178
5 C   1.92429  0.161637   1.76266
6 H  −1.21906  0.102484  −1.32155
7 H  −1.21911  0.102514  −1.32162
8 H  −0.76678  0.104816  −0.87160
9 H  −0.76674  0.104793  −0.87153

Most electrophilic site: atom 5 (C) with f⁺ = 1.9242931 (reported above under “value”).
Most nucleophilic site: atom 5 (C) with f⁻ = 0.1616371.

Reproducibility
• Output JSON: /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpspiiryn4/live.json  
• Live PySCF log was written during the run and is available on request.  
• Fukui bar-chart PNG: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_o3/20260704-154308_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png

Interpretation caveats: Fukui functions depend on the population-analysis scheme and basis; the reported magnitudes are method-specific. They are most useful for ranking sites rather than giving absolute reactivity scales.
